title: Huberia lumiarensis
intro: Huberia lumiarensis is a plant species in the family Melastomataceae, native to Madagascar, known for its medicinal properties and diverse uses. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of H. lumiarensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Malagasy medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or a syrup for various health applications.
